etymology.description: phonetic 釆(釆) (biàn) + semantic 二(二) (èr, "two equal lines") -> flatness, equality
etymology.interpretation: The composition combines 釆(釆) (biàn) serving as a phonetic component, with 二(二) (èr, "two equal lines") providing the idea of equality or flatness. Together, they represent the concept of something being flat or level, as with a balanced surface or peaceful state where everything is even and steady.

examples.chinese: 这个桌面是平的(這個桌面是平的)。
我们的关系一直很平和(我們的關係一直很平和)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen de guānxì yīzhí hěn pínghé.
examples.english: Our relationship has always been peaceful.

examples.chinese: 每天晚上我都享受平静的时光(每天晚上我都享受平靜的時光)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān wǎnshàng wǒ dū xiǎngshòu píngjìng de shíguāng.
examples.english: Every evening I enjoy peaceful times.
